title: fresh juicy tunisian corn tagine
description: a meal-prep tunisian dish featuring dried white corn and a shio-koji-cure
instructions: in a medium bowl, mix together the dried white corn and shio-koji cure. let it sit at room temperature for at least 2 hours, or overnight in the refrigerator.
heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
add the chopped onion and saut5 until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
add the cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
add the soaked and drained corn and its marinade to the skillet, and stir to combine.
add 1 cup of water to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for 20 minutes, or until the corn is tender.
stir in the chopped parsley and lemon juice.
serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
let it cool completely before refrigerating or freezing for later use.
